THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 8, 2011. DEFENDANT TO IMMEDIATELY PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF MOTION TO DEPARTMENT 302. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED

TRIAL MOTION IN DEPT. 206, ATTORNEY CHRISTOPHER B. DOLAN OF THE DOLAN LAW FIRM'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF JOHN KISSINGER. THE HEARING IS CONTINUED FROM FEB-07-2012 TO FEB-10-2012, 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 206. ASSOCIATED COUNSEL DANIEL BERKO, ESQ. APPEARED AND MADE AN ORAL MOTION TO JOIN IN THE DOLAN'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL OF RECORD FOR JOHN KISSINGER. MR. BERKO WAS DIRECTED TO FILE AND SERVE HIS SEPARATE WRITTEN MOTION BY NOON ON FEBRUARY 8, 2012 AND SERVED PERSONALLY ON THE CLIENT. THE COURT ISSUED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS STATED ON THE RECORD WHICH WILL BE ADOPTED IF THERE IS NO APPEARANCE AT THE FEBRUARY 10 HEARING. PARTIES STIPULATE TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE FOR 90 DAYS FROM 2/6/12. ANOTHER HEARING IS SET ON MARCH 13, 2012 AT 9:00AM IN DEPT. 206 FOR PLAINTIFF TO REPORT ON STATUS OF OBTAINING NEW COUNSEL. LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. OFF CALENDAR. MATTER WAS NOTICED FOR HEARING AFTER THE CALENDAR WAS HEARD. (302)
LETTER TO JUDGE MCBRIDE FILED BY PLAINTIFF KISSINGER, JOHN

TRIAL MOTION IN DEPT. 206, ATTORNEY CHRISTOPHER B. DOLAN OF THE DOLAN LAW FIRM'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF JOHN KISSINGER IS CONTINUED FROM FEB-10-2012 TO FEB-27-2012 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 220. ASSOCIATED COUNSEL DANIEL BERKO APPEARED IN COURT. DEWEY WHEELER, COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS, APPEARED IN COURT. PLAINTIFF JOHN KISSINGER APPEARED IN COURT. NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT VACATED THE SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY FILED ON 2/8/12 AND GRANTED MR. BERKO'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FORTHWITH. THE COURT CONTINUED THE HEARING OF THE DOLAN LAW FIRM'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR DECISION BECAUSE OF THE LETTER RECEIVED BY THE COURT FROM MR. KISSINGER. THE COURT DIRECTED MR. KISSINGER TO SEEK NEW COUNSEL AS EXPEDITIOUSLY AS POSSIBLE AND NEW COUNSEL WOULD EITHER FILE AN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO WITHDRAW OR IF THERE IS NO NEW COUNSEL, THEN MR. KISSINGER NEEDS TO FILE AN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION. IF THERE IS NO OPPOSITION FILED, THEN THE DOLAN LAW FIRM'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL WILL BE GRANTED. ALL PROCEEDINGS IN THE CASE ARE STAYED AND DISCOVERY IS CLOSED. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERED MR. BERKO TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER AND SERVE PERSONALLY TO THE DOLAN'S LAW FIRM BY CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON 2/13/12. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Independent Mental Examination Of Pltf
Off Calendar Sep-08-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JUNE GADDIS'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MENTAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF.
Appearances: Quinton B. Cutlip of the Dolan Law Firm (421.2800) for plaintiff and responding party; Tanner D. Brink of McNamara Ney Beatty Slattery Borges & Ambacher LLP (925.939.5330) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant's motion to compel an independent mental exam of plaintiff is granted. The claim for cognitive injury was recently revealed to defendant and the motion is timely. Plaintiff's request that no questions be asked about the accident itself is ordered. Plaintiff's other proposed limitations are denied. The examination will take place during a full day with appropriate breaks. The examiner can ask about plaintiff's symptoms and treatment. Further, the court ordered the report to be completed within 3 days of the examination. Defendant to submit a proposed order that lists which tests are to be administered. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
